tasks.title: Extract validation logic to reusable validators
evidence: Each validator has similar pattern: ValidateURL, ValidateMaxDepth, ValidateMaxPages, ValidateTimeout, ValidateAuthProfileName
Scheduler and API handlers likely duplicate similar validation logic
Repeated validation patterns violate DRY principle
plan: Read validate/request.go to understand current validator implementations
Read scheduler.go to find validation logic in validateSchedule()
Read api/server.go to find validation logic in handlers
Identify common validation rules and create reusable functions: ValidateJobCommon(opts JobCommonOpts)
Create internal/validate/job.go with shared validation logic
Update scheduler to use shared validators
Update API handlers to use shared validators
Ensure MCP tool handlers use shared validators
Run make test to verify all validation tests pass
Run make ci to ensure full pipeline works

tasks.title: Verify generated TypeScript API types match Go API contracts
evidence: API contract is api/openapi.yaml and generates TS client via 'make generate' (hey-api openapi-ts)
Potential drift between Go server types and generated TS types
No automated verification that generated types match actual API responses
Type mismatches cause runtime errors in web client
plan: Read api/openapi.yaml to understand the API contract
Read internal/api/server.go to identify all request/response types
Compare Go types with OpenAPI spec for consistency
Read generated types in web/src/api/types.gen.ts
Verify all API endpoints and types are correctly represented in OpenAPI spec
Add a test or script that validates API responses against OpenAPI schema
Consider adding 'make verify-api' target to check type consistency
Run make generate to ensure regeneration works
Run make ci to ensure full pipeline works

tasks.title: Review config.Load() singleton pattern for thread safety
evidence: config.Load() is called once in cli.go and config object is passed around
Search found only 1 call to config.Load() in the entire codebase
No evidence of concurrent access issues, but config could be accessed from multiple goroutines (scheduler, manager)
Config object should be immutable or thread-safe if shared across goroutines
plan: Read internal/config/config.go to understand Config struct and its fields
Check if Config struct is modified after Load() returns
Trace config usage across scheduler, manager, api to identify write patterns
If config is modified after initialization, make Config immutable (read-only fields) or add mutex
Document config access patterns in AGENTS.md or config.go package docs
Add tests that verify config thread safety if mutations are possible
Run make test to verify tests pass
Run make ci to ensure full pipeline works
